_note: Self-Care starter set for The GuyTalk Guide. Two picks (Le Labo Santal 33, Dior Sauvage EDT) already have real detail pages under /guide/style/ from the Style category — self-care links straight to those instead of duplicating content. The rest get their own /guide/self-care/<slug>/ pages via scripts/generate-guide-detail-pages.js.
categoryTagline: The grooming and skincare that actually moves the needle.

picks.name: Eye Repair Cream
picks.bio: The one product that noticeably changes how tired your face looks. Caffeine reduces puffiness, peptides firm the delicate skin around your eyes, and hyaluronic acid keeps it hydrated. Use it every morning and people will ask what you did differently.
picks.flashyCheck: Zero flash — nobody clocks an eye cream. They just notice you look less tired.

picks.name: Facial Moisturizing Lotion AM/PM
picks.bio: Hydrating without being heavy. Three essential ceramides and hyaluronic acid replace what water strips from your skin. The AM version has SPF 30. Use this twice a day and your skin stops looking like it needs attention. It just looks balanced.
picks.flashyCheck: None. This is baseline maintenance, not a flex.

picks.name: Facial Fuel Moisturizer
picks.bio: If CeraVe isn't cutting it and you want something richer. This is designed for men — it absorbs fast, doesn't clog pores, and the fuel complex actually energizes your face. A little goes a long way. Use after shaving to prevent irritation.
picks.flashyCheck: Low-key. Guys who know skincare recognize the tube; nobody else notices.

picks.name: Men+Care Body Wash
picks.bio: Stop using whatever's in the shower. This doesn't strip your skin or leave it tight. It's formulated for men's skin, it actually smells clean rather than artificial, and it's cheap enough to use without guilt. The bar version is equally good.
picks.flashyCheck: None, and that's fine — this is a baseline upgrade, not a statement piece.

picks.name: Men Face Wash
picks.bio: A solid daily face wash that doesn't over-dry. Removes dirt and oil without stripping your skin. Use it morning and night before moisturizer. Not fancy. Just effective. This is the baseline — everything else is an upgrade from here.
picks.flashyCheck: None. This is the floor everyone should already be standing on.

picks.name: Clay Pomade
picks.bio: Holds without shine or flake. The clay formula gives you control without the wet look that screams product. Works on all hair types and actually conditions while it styles. Rinses clean in one wash. This is the move if you're tired of pomade feeling sticky all day.
picks.flashyCheck: Safe. Matte hold reads as effortless, not styled.

picks.name: Shaving Cream
picks.bio: Made for men's beards. Creates a thick, protective lather that reduces irritation and ingrown hairs. Works with any razor but optimized for straight razors. Single ingredient: not packed with chemicals. Your skin will thank you.
picks.flashyCheck: None. Purely functional, which is the point.

picks.name: Bain Energisant Shampoo
picks.bio: Not cheap shampoo from the grocery store. This is designed to strengthen hair and add volume. Cleans without stripping. Pairs with conditioner. Your hair stops looking like it needs work and starts looking intentional. Worth the upgrade.
picks.flashyCheck: Low-key. A salon-tier shampoo nobody sees the bottle for.

picks.name: Beard Oil
picks.bio: If you're growing a beard, this stops it from looking rough and unkempt. Conditions the hair without making it greasy. A few drops in the beard after showering keeps it soft and prevents itching. Not a scent product — designed to fade into the background.
picks.flashyCheck: None by design — it's meant to be invisible, not noticed.

picks.name: Santal 33 Cologne
picks.bio: Warm, smoky sandalwood that smells expensive and intentional. It's on many guys in major cities, which means it's genuinely great. If you love it, wear it — don't worry about standing out. One bottle lasts a year.
picks.flashyCheck: This IS the flex — Le Labo at $240 a bottle is expensive niche fragrance, though wearing it in a big city now reads more like a uniform than a statement.

picks.name: Sauvage EDT Cologne
picks.bio: The most popular men's fragrance in the world for a reason — fresh, peppery, universally liked. It's not original, but it's never wrong. Apply 2-3 sprays on skin, not clothes. This is the safest first bottle a guy can own.
picks.flashyCheck: Safe, mainstream — everyone knows it, which makes it a reliable default, not a statement.

picks.name: Aventus Cologne
picks.bio: The reference fragrance. If you're only going to invest in one expensive cologne, make it this. Pineapple and birch top notes give it brightness; ambroxan base keeps it grounded. It smells intentional. A bottle lasts two years of daily wear.
picks.flashyCheck: This is the flex — $380 for a bottle is a real statement, but it reads as taste, not showing off, because most people can't place why it smells so good.

picks.name: Daily Facial Cleanser
picks.bio: If your skin is reactive or easily irritated, this is your baseline. No fragrance, no sulfates, no stripping. Dermatologist-tested. It cleans without aggravating. Pair it with a good moisturizer and you're covered.
picks.flashyCheck: None. Purely functional skincare, no signaling involved.
